questions.questionText: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) is based on the principle that:
questions.optionB: Maladaptive cognitions (negative automatic thoughts, cognitive distortions) lead to emotional distress and dysfunctional behavior — modifying these improves outcomes
questions.explanation: CBT: evidence-based psychotherapy for depression, anxiety, OCD, PTSD, eating disorders. Core concept: cognitive triad (Beck) — negative automatic thoughts about self, world, and future drive depression. Cognitive distortions: all-or-nothing thinking, catastrophizing, mind reading, overgeneralization. Techniques: cognitive restructuring (identify and challenge negative thoughts), behavioral activation, exposure therapy (for anxiety/phobias), homework assignments. Time-limited (12-20 sessions typically). Evidence: first-line for MDD (mild-moderate) + anxiety disorders + OCD. Reference: Niraj Ahuja — CBT.
